description_long: This helmet is a robust, full-head covering made primarily of dark, matte-finished metal, reminiscent of medieval European great helms. The front features a prominent vertical and horizontal metallic cross reinforcement, providing both structural strength and a distinctive visual element. The eye slit is narrow and rectangular, offering limited but focused visibility for the wearer. The sides and rear are smooth and rounded, with no visible openings, decorations, or attachments, emphasizing protection over ventilation or comfort. The surface shows subtle texturing and a slightly weathered appearance, suggesting durability and use. There are no visible logos, graphics, or modern features, giving the helmet a utilitarian, historical look.
description: A dark, metal medieval-style helmet with a cross-shaped reinforcement on the front and a narrow eye slit, featuring a smooth, rounded surface and no visible modern features.
description_view_0: Front view shows the cross-shaped metallic reinforcement and the narrow eye slit.
description_view_1: Front-side view highlights the thickness of the metal and the attachment points of the cross reinforcement.
description_view_2: Rear view displays a smooth, unadorned, rounded metal surface with no openings.
description_view_3: Back-side view reveals the helmet's continuous curvature and the side profile of the cross reinforcement.
